title: verbascum × semialbum
intro: Verbascum × semialbum, also known as the semi-double mullein, is a hybrid plant species within the verbascum genus, characterized by its large, showy flowers and tall, upright growth habit. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which make it useful in treating conditions such as bronchitis, sore throats, and skin irritations. The health benefits of Verbascum × semialbum include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and promote wound healing, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or topical salve, and its leaves, flowers, and roots are often used in herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
